EUR.EUR/USD: The euro is supported by a significant improvement in the July Sentix survey and a surge in German factory orders, though structurally capped by a +150bp Fed-ECB carry disadvantage. ECB policymakers Joachim Nagel and Moulin have signaled a "hawkish hold" stance. EUR/USD at 1.1446; ECB 2.25% vs Fed 3.75% — 150bp carry favors USD
EUR.EUR/GBP: The cross is influenced by the EUR CESI at -11, which continues to lag the U.S. fundamental profile, and the GBP's critical technical test near its 200-day SMA. ECB’s Panetta has signaled no predetermined path for rates
EUR.EUR/JPY: The pair is impacted by the yen's 40-year low against the dollar, keeping markets on high alert for Ministry of Finance intervention, and the persistent +125bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. JPY COT LF short −137,828
EUR.EUR/AUD: The Australian dollar is pressured by cooler domestic inflation and weak employment data, while corporate activity provided a localized catalyst with Genesis Minerals' bid for Vault Minerals. AUD (LEADER) +0.612% vs JPY (LAGGARD) −0.427% — G10 bilateral theme
EUR.EUR/CAD: The Canadian dollar is trading near 1.4210 against the USD, pressured by uncertainty surrounding the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) renewal deadline and CAD COT LF short -92.9k
EUR.EUR/CHF: The Swiss franc is underperforming as the U.S. dollar captures safe-haven flows, with USD/CHF aiming for the 0.8065 area amid broad greenback strength
EUR.EUR/NZD: The New Zealand dollar is facing acute volatility ahead of the July 8 Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) policy meeting, with NZD/USD COT LF net short −25,757

GBP.GBP/USD: Sterling is facing a critical technical test near its 200-day SMA as market participants weigh a disappointing S&P Global Construction PMI of 38.4 for June against hawkish rhetoric from Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann. GBP/USD 25d RR at −0.67 — mild put skew signals options market biased for GBP weakness
